mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows) or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to discount future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during structural labor shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in consumer price inflation and the federal funds rate indicate aggressive monetary tightening to combat price pressures, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both rise, with positive 20-day returns occurring in more than 60% of cases, or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rapid month-over-month expansions in industrial production reflect supply chain normalization and rising corporate output, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery regimes, with positive returns occurring in less than 45% of high-growth months, or the mechanism reverses direction during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term consumer credit balances indicate expanding household purchasing power and consumption demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth shows no predictive relationship with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows) or positive returns occur in less than 50% of credit expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month payroll employment decline exceeding 200,000 signals acute labor market stress and weakening aggregate demand, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 200k in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 yields positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of months following a greater than 200k PAYEMS drop, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy during periods of seasonal adjustment revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and consumer price inflation reflect strong real estate demand and broad-based price momentum, which historically coincides with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as equity markets price in sustained economic activity.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and CPIAUCSL uptrend fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with negative returns occurring in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when housing supply constraints dominate.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion regimes, quarterly GDP growth exceeding 4% annualized signals an overheating economy that forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 4% fails to predict negative 60-day returns during late-cycle regimes, with positive returns occurring in more than 65% of instances, or the signal becomes positive during early-cycle phases.
